purpose: Validate that low-risk work avoids unnecessary agent/tool expansion.

cases.userPrompt: Use governance. Fix one typo in README.md.
cases.expectedResponseShape: Short routing summary, file changed, validation note.

cases.userPrompt: Use governance. Add a link to the roadmap in the docs README.
Use governance. Explain what the audit profile is for.
cases.expectedResponseShape: Direct explanation with file reference; no registry dump.

cases.userPrompt: Use governance. Fix a misspelled word in the token efficiency policy.
expectedStopConditions: Edit changes policy meaning or scope
cases.expectedResponseShape: One or two sentences with file changed and diff/static validation.

cases.userPrompt: Use governance. Show the full token efficiency policy.
cases.expectedResponseShape: Full policy content is allowed because the user explicitly asked for it.

cases.userPrompt: Use governance. Audit all important sources, tools, agents, profiles, and token risks before implementation.
Review this PR using only the relevant changed files and dependency neighborhood.
Create a context pack for a large review, but do not expose private overlays or secrets.
Continue the old audit from the saved graph and say whether it is still valid.
expectedContextGovernance: reduced confidence if refresh cannot run
forbiddenClaims: saved graph is current without verification
